behavior_summary: This script implements a simple 'U8P_LOTHLAY::look' handler that shows the NPC name "Lothian" when examined, and a hit-reaction handler that prevents duplicate processing, validates an attacker, emits a panic bark, triggers camera/sound feedback, and forwards the hit to the damage resolution wrapper.
Duplicate-hit protection is implemented via an item/state Q flag (checked with Item::getQ and set with Item::setQ).

When a valid attacker parameter is present and is an alive NPC, the NPC reacts: a bark/play-sound, a brief suspend, Camera::startQuake, playSFX, then a call to METHOD::receiveHitWrapper with computed HP and direction; finally Camera::stopQuake is invoked.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LOTHLAY::look: single-line flow — emit Bark("Lothian") and suspend (process ends).
U8P_LOTHLAY::gotHit: early-return guard if Item::getQ(this) indicates already-processed; otherwise set Item::setQ(0x01,this) to mark processing.
U8P_LOTHLAY::gotHit then validates param1: must be non-zero, Item::isNpc(param1) true, and Npc::isDead(param1) false — only then do audiovisual reaction and damage forwarding.
Visual/sound feedback sequence: Bark -> suspend -> Camera::startQuake -> playSFX -> [receive hit wrapper call] -> Camera::stopQuake.
open_questions: Exact meanings of U8P_LOTHLAY::gotHit parameters (uword, word) — which parameter is the attacker reference versus numeric damage/context (inferred but not certain).
Purpose of the observed spawn->FREE::func1C19 comparison and the 'pid' token in the decompilation — their role in timing or spawn checks is unclear.
Precise numeric meanings of the literal arguments (e.g., 0x01, 0x00C8, 0x31) used with Item::setQ/playSFX/Camera::startQuake — likely flags/IDs but not fully resolved.

main_function.name: U8P_LOTHLAY::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines this NPC — emits the NPC name "Lothian" and ends the U8P_LOTHLAY::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine on a U8P_LOTHLAY::look/examine event for the NPC (process type 0x0208).
No additional local conditions observed in the function.

function_map.name: U8P_LOTHLAY::look
function_map.role: present NPC short description on player 'U8P_LOTHLAY::look' (Bark "Lothian").

function_map.name: U8P_LOTHLAY::gotHit
function_map.role: handle incoming hit events for this item/NPC: duplicate-hit guard, attacker validation, audiovisual reaction, and forward hit to damage wrapper.

external_calls.purpose: Read per-item/state Q flag to detect whether this hit has already been processed (duplicate-hit guard).
Set per-item/state Q flag (observed value 0x01) to mark the hit as processed and prevent reprocessing.
Validate that a provided parameter refers to an NPC before NPC-specific reaction logic proceeds.
Check that the attacker NPC is not dead; if dead, skip reaction and damage forwarding.
Start a camera quake effect to emphasize the impact/hit.
Play a hit/alert sound (observed IDs 0x00C8 and 0x31 used as arguments).
Forward/apply the hit to the target; called with the target HP (Npc::getHp), a direction computed by Item::getDirFromItem, the item instance, and the attacker reference.
Stop the camera quake effect after hit handling completes.
Obtain the target NPC's current HP to supply to the receiveHitWrapper call.
Compute hit direction from the item (used as an argument to receiveHitWrapper).
Appears in a comparison/timing/spawn-related check before hit application; exact role is unclear.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a simple 'U8P_LOTHLAY::look' handler that shows the NPC name "Lothian" when examined, and a hit-reaction handler that prevents duplicate processing, validates an attacker, emits a panic bark, triggers camera/sound feedback, and forwards the hit to the damage resolution wrapper.
